artifacts.content:
From: mthompson@thompson-pm.com
To: emily.hart@gmail.com
Subject: Sophie, Lakeside results, and the week ahead
Date: 2026-03-02

Em,

I’m trying to sketch out this week so we’re not totally underwater.

Big one: I really need the Lakeside lab report back before Sophie’s Lurie follow‑up with Dr. Karen Weiss on 3/12. I keep replaying that asthma flare she had after I brought her into the warehouse on 2/10 – she was only in there for a few minutes while I checked the roof leaks and boiler room, and we were up half the night with the inhaler. Karen was pretty clear she wants the asbestos and lead results in hand so we can talk about any long‑term exposure risk.

Laura Chen was on site 2/25 pulling the asbestos bulk PLM and lead chips – boiler insulation, those 9x9 tiles, the roof core, plus the steel window frames and CMU wall in the loading dock. I asked her to have ACM Engineering email the PDF straight to me at mthompson@thompson-pm.com and cc her so I can forward it to Karen before the appointment.

On top of that, I’ve got the Lakeside budgeting meeting 3/5, Caleb’s indoor soccer Friday, Sophie’s jazz rehearsal runs early this week, and Mom wants us to come out to Oak Park Saturday to look at grab bars after her fall. I’m thinking: I’ll handle the Thursday coding club pickup if you can cover Sophie’s 7:30 a.m. band run on Friday so I can swing by the Lakeside site before the office.

Also, remind me tonight to block time to actually read the lab report before we’re sitting in the exam room at Lurie.

– M

metadata.subject: Sophie, Lakeside results, and the week ahead

artifacts.content:
From: mthompson@thompson-pm.com
To: richard.thompson47@yahoo.com
Subject: Env. reports, Jenner & Block, and money stuff
Date: 2026-03-06

Dad,

Quick update before the day gets away from me.

ACM Engineering finally sent over the full asbestos/lead package for the former Lakeside Manufacturing Facility (3920 S Ashland). It’s the one Laura Chen sampled on 2/25 – boiler room pipe and elbow insulation, 9x9 VAT, roof core, plus the lead chips from the steel windows and CMU wall in the loading dock. I asked them to keep all the raw data and QA/QC documentation organized because I know you want to sit down with the attorney at Jenner & Block the week of 3/16 to see if there’s any angle against the seller over the October 2025 closing and the prior abatement they “forgot” to mention.

Same report is going into the refinancing package I’m submitting to Lakeshore Community Bank today to restructure about $1.2M in high‑interest bridge debt. It’s not just the Ashland warehouse – the two Logan Square dogs that have been cash‑flow negative for the last nine months are rolled into that note too. I’m trying to convince Lakeshore to give us some breathing room before rates tick up again.

Between that, Mom’s fall and all the Oak Park runs, and juggling Sophie’s Lurie follow‑up on 3/12, I’m feeling stretched thin. But if we can leverage the env. data both for the refi and for a potential claim on the seller, it might finally move us out of constant triage.

Let me know which day that week works for you to go over everything at Jenner – I’ll block it on my calendar.

– Mike

metadata.subject: Env. reports, Jenner & Block, and money stuff

artifacts.content:
From: mthompson@thompson-pm.com
To: melissa.grant@grantlawchicago.com
Subject: Lab timing before 3/20 Daley hearing
Date: 2026-03-01

Melissa,

Circling back on the code enforcement case for the Lakeside warehouse.

Laura Chen finished the pre‑renovation hazardous materials survey on 2/25 – asbestos bulk PLM on the pipe and boiler insulation, 9x9 floor tile and mastic, gypsum board/joint compound, plus the roof core, and lead paint chip analysis for the original steel window frames and interior CMU wall in the loading dock. ACM Engineering has the samples and promised a 2–3 day turnaround, but we’re already pushing that.

I stressed to them that turnaround is critical because of the 3/20 status hearing at the Richard J. Daley Center. You’d mentioned wanting the asbestos and lead results in hand so we can show the City we’re proactively addressing the hazardous materials issues they raised in the 11/03/2025 violation notice. I also asked them to flag any friable thermal system insulation that looks fibrous even if PLM comes back non‑detect so we can decide about TEM.

In parallel, I’ve told vendors to email me rather than call the office line if anything sensitive pops up. Daniel’s been helping at the office since he got his real estate license back after that 6‑month suspension in spring 2024 for the ethics violation, and he sometimes answers my phone. Last thing I need is him overhearing something that spooks the carrier before our May 2026 insurance renewal.

Kids are fine – Sophie’s buried in jazz band and Caleb’s living in a LEGO city – but I’d really like this hearing not to blow up my spring.

Let me know if there’s anything specific you want me to prod the lab about.

Best,
Michael

Title: Running list – environmental landmines & family stuff
Date: 2026-02-28

– Lakeside (3920 S Ashland): Laura Chen sampled 2/25. Need asbestos PLM on boiler insulation (L‑06, L‑07) + roof core (L‑10) and lead chips (L‑08, L‑09) back before:
   • 3/5 budgeting meeting with lender
   • 3/6 Lakeshore Community Bank refi package (restructure ~$1.2M bridge debt on Lakeside + 2 Logan Sq. dogs, 9 months cash‑flow negative)
   • 3/12 Lurie Children’s follow‑up with Dr. Karen Weiss re: Sophie’s asthma flare after 2/10 warehouse visit.

– Dad wants full asbestos/lead package (incl. raw data/QA/QC) to review with attorney at Jenner & Block week of 3/16 in case we push seller over October 2025 closing / nondisclosed abatement.

– City code case: 3/20 status hearing at Daley Center. Melissa Grant needs lab report to show we’re addressing 11/3/25 violation. Don’t let this slip.

– Office comms: remind myself – no sensitive env. stuff over office line. Daniel sometimes grabs the phone; after his 6‑month license suspension in spring 2024 for that ethics mess, I don’t want him hearing anything that could complicate May 2026 insurance renewal.

– Home front: Sophie’s spring concert late March (jazz solo), Caleb’s soccer Fridays, Mom’s fall in Oak Park still rattling her. Try to get at least one run on the 606 this week so I don’t show up to Lurie and Daley looking like I’ve aged 10 years.

– Long term: keep asking whether Lakeside scale is worth the stress.

metadata.title: Running list – environmental landmines & family stuff

From: mthompson@thompson-pm.com
To: emily.hart@gmail.com
Subject: Please don’t let Daniel pick up env. calls
Date: 2026-02-24

Em,

Random request but important: if you’re at the office and the phone rings from any environmental lab or consultant, please either let it go to voicemail or just take a message and text me. I’ve started telling people to email instead of calling about anything sensitive.

Daniel’s been good about helping at the front desk, but after his 6‑month license suspension in spring 2024 for that ethics violation, I really don’t want him overhearing details about asbestos, lead, or potential claims that could come back up when the carrier looks at us for the May 2026 insurance renewal. He means well, he just doesn’t need that stress either.

This week is already stacked: Laura Chen is sampling the former Lakeside Manufacturing Facility on Ashland tomorrow (2/25) – boiler room, 9x9 floor tile, roof core, steel windows, CMU wall in the loading dock. I’ve got to juggle that with Sophie’s early jazz rehearsal, Caleb’s shoe hunt, and your Lincoln Park clinic shift.

On top of that, I’ve still got Dad in my ear about lining up a meeting at Jenner & Block the week of 3/16 once the asbestos/lead results are in, and Lakeshore Community Bank breathing down my neck for the 3/6 refi package on the $1.2M bridge debt.

So yeah, fewer surprises on the office line would be nice.

Thanks,
M

metadata.subject: Please don’t let Daniel pick up env. calls

artifacts.content:
Title: Late‑night brain dump – Lakeside, money, kids
Date: 2026-03-04

Couldn’t sleep, so putting this somewhere instead of spinning.

Lakeside is starting to feel like the fulcrum for everything. If the asbestos PLM on the boiler insulation or roof core comes back ugly, the abatement budget blows up, I have to redo the pro forma before the 3/5 meeting, and the 3/6 Lakeshore refi on the $1.2M bridge debt (plus the two Logan Square losers that have bled cash for 9 months) gets shaky. If I can’t restructure, I’m back to robbing Peter to pay Paul.

At the same time, Sophie’s lungs are literally part of this. I brought her into that warehouse on 2/10 for ten minutes and she was wheezing that night. Now we’re heading to Lurie on 3/12 to sit in front of Dr. Weiss and talk about “long‑term exposure risk.” Hard not to feel like I’m choosing between growth and being a decent dad.

Dad’s already lining up Jenner & Block for the week of 3/16 to see if we can use the asbestos/lead data against the seller on the October 2025 closing. Melissa’s prepping for the 3/20 Daley Center hearing on the 11/3/25 violation. Everyone wants the same lab report for a different fire.

And in the middle of it, I’m worrying about Daniel’s suspension history and the May 2026 insurance renewal, Mom’s fall, Emily’s clinic hours, Caleb’s soccer, and whether I’ll ever get back to running the 606 more than once a week.

Feels like too many plates in the air for one small company.

metadata.title: Late‑night brain dump – Lakeside, money, kids

Laura,

Per our call yesterday, I pulled together a consolidated description of the recent samples so your team can mirror the IDs in your chain-of-custody.

  • TPM-2637NDamen-BoilerElbow-PostTenantMoldComplaint-Unit2F-SophieCough-02/27/2024 – collected after the Unit 2F tenant’s mold/asthma complaint and Sophie’s lingering cough.

2) Armitage / prior DIY work:
- Basement mechanical room, 1842 W Armitage Ave – approx. 650 sq ft, same deteriorated pipe insulation Dad and I disturbed during the late‑2019 DIY boiler replacement that his Northwestern pulmonologist links to his chronic cough (see your 2025‑02‑06 bulk sample notes).

3) Roof cores – 2637 N Damen, Unit 2F (our residence):
- MT-ROOFCORE-10/12/2024-EMERGENCY-CLAIM#22-4879-STATEFARM-MOLD/ASBESTOS-2637NDAMEN-UNIT2F-POST-EMILY-ER-VISIT – emergency roof core after Emily’s October 2024 ER visit.
- TPG-LLC-ROOFCORE-2637NDAMEN-UNIT2F-CODEVIOLATION-NO.2024-1103-CHICAGOBUILDINGDEPT – core tied to the November 2024 DOB violation 2024‑1103.
- 2637NDAMEN-2F-ROOFCORE-SOPHIE-CHRONIC-COUGH-PEDIATRICIAN-DRKLEIN-09/2024 – core requested by Dr. Klein re: Sophie’s chronic cough.
- MT-ROOFCORE-2637NDAMEN-2F-POST-FORECLOSURE-NOTICE-CHASE-LOAN#7842-08/2024 – documentation for the Chase foreclosure notice on loan #7842.

Let me know if you need clarifications on locations or square footages.

Subject: Kids’ health, paint chips, and that damn basement

Em,

I know you’re exhausted after Sophie’s ADHD/lead follow‑up at Lurie Children’s today, but I wanted you to have a written rundown of what I actually sampled so we’re not trying to reconstruct it at midnight next week.

At our place (2637 N Damen, Unit 2F), I pulled:

- Approx. 3 sq in of chipping baseboard paint from the north wall of the kids’ play nook (also 2–4 ft above the floor) that DCFS asked about after Caleb’s playground fall and ER visit at Swedish last November.

- Approx. 4 sq in of layered chips from the shared kids’ bedroom window frame above Caleb’s bunk – the condensation/peeling spot Lakeview Heating flagged in October 2023 for possible asbestos‑containing joint compound.
- Approx. 4 sq in of peeling chips from Sophie’s old nursery window (same unit), which I grabbed on 3/18/2024 after Lurie first flagged a mild lead bump.

I’ll scan and upload all the lab COCs tomorrow so you can forward them to the doctors if they ask.

Last updated: 2025-02-20 – M.R.T.

1) Kids’ asthma / ER trigger points (Damen 2637):
- Basement boiler room, 180 sq ft high‑risk zone with two 8" elbows and ~25 LF wrap where Caleb’s asthma attack hit on 10/05/2023 – Dr. Patel at Lurie said treat as high‑risk until asbestos results (MS95).
- Utility closet behind Sophie’s bedroom, 8" elbow grab sample taken weekend of 01/13–01/14/2024 after she told the Pulaski International counselor she was scared of “asbestos dust” (MS97).
- Boiler insulation directly under kids’ bedrooms, ~80 sq ft exposed fibrous wrap Emily and I have worried about since Sophie’s Feb 2024 flare (MS83).
- Boiler elbow insulation sample logged as MRThompson-2637NDamen-BoilerRm-ElbowInsul-PostCalebAsthmaER-08/14/2024, taken days after Caleb’s 08/10/2024 ER visit (MS84).

2) Lead / window hotspots tied to kids + city:
- LEAD-CHIP-SOPHIE-BEDROOM-WINDOW-2637NDAMEN-2F-2024-01-05 – dark‑green steel window in Sophie’s room post 01/05/2024 elevated lead test (MS99).
- LEAD-CHIP-2637NDAMEN-APT2F-WINDOW-RICHARD-VA-INSPECT-2023-08-14 – same window frame Dad flagged during Mom’s VA loan inspection (MS98).
- LEAD-CHIP-2637NDAMEN-2F-WINDOW-EVICTION-NOTICE-2023-11-02 – chip taken after serving the 5‑day notice to the 2F tenant (MS100).
- LEAD-CHIP-2637NDAMEN-2F-WINDOW-CITYVIOLATION-CASE2024-00117-2024-02-19 – tied to DOB lead case 2024‑00117 (MS101).
- LEAD-CHIP-2637NDAMEN-2F-WINDOW-CALEB-ASTHMA-ERFOLLOWUP-2023-09-28 – per Dr. Nguyen’s request after Caleb’s 09/28/2023 asthma ER visit (MS102).

3) Basement/mech historical exposures:
- Armitage illegal basement unit – ~480 sq ft 9"x9" VAT left under laminate per May 2021 consent agreement (MS137).
- Logan Sq storage – ~200 sq ft, 18" deep loose vermiculite in Dan’s unit at 2411 W Fullerton discovered Sept 2023 while moving Erica’s Swedish Hospital records (MS136).
- 3F nursery ceiling at 2637 – ~120 sq ft, 2" joint compound layer where Sophie’s crib used to be; home kit showed lead after her March 2022 Lurie checkup (MS135).

Laura,

Following up on your February 25 visit to the Lakeside Manufacturing Facility and on the various condo and small-building issues I mentioned on the phone, I’m pulling together all the sample IDs and locations so your chain-of-custody matches what my attorneys, doctors, and lenders already have in their files.

Laura,

At our home/office building, 2637 N Damen Ave (we live in Unit 2F), I’ve used a few different serials over the years:

• For estate planning with my parents, Margaret “Maggie” Thompson and Richard Thompson, I pulled a basement pipe sample on 2637 in January 2024 and labeled it THOMPSON-FAMILY-TRUST-ESTATE-PLAN-BASEMENT-PIPE-01-2024-S1 so the asbestos result could sit with the Thompson Family Trust environmental liability section.

• Right after my March 14, 2023 DUI hearing at the Daley Center with Judge Hernandez, I met Luis Ramirez at 2637 and grabbed a confirmatory pipe sample in the basement mechanical room, labeled MR-2637-DUI-PIPE-02-OF-02-03/14/2023 to mark it against the 12‑month restricted driving permit period.

From: Michael Robert Thompson <mthompson@thompsonprop.com>

Date: 2025-03-06 11:19 CST
Subject: Environmental documentation across portfolio – for litigation/claims files

Alan,

Ahead of any new tenant injury or habitability claims, I’m trying to centralize how we describe area/volume/material and sample locations.

Examples:

• At 1142 W. Armitage (12‑unit), Daniel and I exposed about 6 LF of 2" hot‑water return pipe in the basement laundry during a January 2025 emergency leak after Mrs. Alvarez in Unit 1B reported recurring mold behind her washer. That’s the section we documented under the “Area Volume Material” line for the lab.

• At 317 W. Huron St. (Mom’s transitional housing after her fall in November 2024), I logged approximately 6 LF of 2" domestic hot water pipe insulation over the shower stall she uses daily in the basement, again under Area/Volume/Material.

• For my sister‑in‑law/tenant Erica Thompson at 890 W. Wilson Ave., we noted roughly 6 LF of 2" heating pipe wrap in the back hallway ceiling outside 3F, directly where she said her asthma worsened in October 2024 when the radiators kicked on.

• For the foreclosure rehab at 1814 W Armitage, Apt 3R, tied to the $22,000 mold lawsuit settlement with Jessica Nguyen in May 2021, we documented approx. 600 sq ft of 9" x 9" VAT in the living room (composite of 3 tiles plus mastic) as part of the hazmat survey.

• Similarly, in our own Unit 2F at 2637 N Damen, after Dad’s January 2024 hip fracture on the loose VAT tiles, we logged approx. 600 sq ft of 9" x 9" VAT in the hallway and took a composite of 3 tiles plus mastic.

I want future COC forms for places like 915 W 18th (eviction/mold) and 2015 W North (roof ACM/code plan) to mirror this level of detail so your office isn’t chasing context later.

Michael

metadata.subject: Environmental documentation across portfolio – for litigation/claims files

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Robert Thompson <mthompson@thompsonprop.com>
To: Karen Doyle <kdoyle@midwestlending.com>
Cc: Emily Hart-Thompson <emily.hart@northwestern.org>
Date: 2025-03-07 07:54 CST
Subject: Example of serials/locations for environmental due diligence – 2637 N Damen & related

Karen,

For the refi and the Lakeside Manufacturing Facility loan, you asked how we track environmental issues. Below are representative serials and locations from 2637 N Damen and a few related properties to show our documentation style.

Loading dock / lead & mold:
• LEAD-CHIP-EMILY-PREGNANCY-SCARE-11-07-2022-DOCK-WALL – light grey CMU paint chip from the loading dock interior wall where my wife, Emily Hart‑Thompson, complained of strong dust/paint fumes during a November 7, 2022 pregnancy scare.
• LEAD-CHIP-MAGGIE-ER-05-02-2023-LOADING-DOCK – grey paint chip from the same dock wall where my mother, Margaret “Maggie” Thompson, scraped her hand on 05/02/2023 and ended up in the Northwestern Memorial ER.
• LEAD-CHIP-CODE-VIOLATION-FINE-3500-01-10-2024-LOADING-DOCK – chip taken after the City issued a $3,500 lead‑related code violation at 2637 on 01/10/2024.
• For Caleb’s October 18, 2024 asthma flare at the first‑floor loading dock (ER at Lurie Children’s), Dr. Patel asked us to test the east wall near the overhead door; that’s the same location State Farm is now reviewing for the January 5, 2025 Unit 2F pipe‑burst staining and where tenant Daniel Ortiz slipped on November 3, 2024 and threatened to sue Thompson Property Management if mold/asbestos tests are bad.

Warehouse / VAT & PCB:
• MT-EMILY-POST-CHEMO-BASEMENT-2637-ASB-02 – 9" x 9" brown tile with black mastic from the first‑floor warehouse, collected 10/02/2024 right after Emily’s last chemo.
• MT-CODE-VIOLATION-ABATEMENT-CHICAGO-2637-ASB-TILE-04 – warehouse VAT sample tied to the 07/22/2024 Chicago DOB asbestos abatement plan and $1,750 fine.
• PCB-ROOF-2637NDAMEN-MAGGIE-INVEST-08-2023 – roof drain sample after Mom threatened to report a PCB leak to the City in August 2023.

This level of specificity (dates, locations, events) is what we’ll replicate for Lakeside and 1840 W North.

Michael

metadata.subject: Example of serials/locations for environmental due diligence – 2637 N Damen & related

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Thompson <m.thompson@thompsonpmllc.com>
To: Laura Chen <lchen@midwestenviro.com>
Cc: Daniel Thompson <daniel@thompsonpmllc.com>
Date: March 2, 2026
Subject: Consolidated sampling notes before 3/5 Lakeside budget meeting

Laura,

Ahead of Thursday’s Lakeside Manufacturing budget meeting, I pulled together a single summary of the higher‑risk sampling locations so we can talk realistically about abatement exposure.

On the family side:
- Parents’ Naperville place: I still want one confirmatory grab from the basement mechanical room at my parents’ house in Naperville, along the east wall by the condensate tank where we ripped out the old asbestos‑wrapped piping right after Dad’s lung cancer diagnosis in October 2022. That experience is exactly why I’m touchy about mechanical rooms.
- Mom’s condo/health issues: We already sampled the first‑floor south elevation center bay steel window frame in her living room at 5200 N Sheridan Rd after Margaret “Maggie” Thompson’s COPD hospitalization in October 2023, and I also took about 4 sq in of chipping paint from the bathroom window frame above the tub at 1124 W Foster Ave on 2/2/24, where she slipped and broke her wrist last fall. Those are for her pulmonologist Dr. Reyes and the Medicare supplemental insurer, but they inform how I’m thinking about risk.

On my own buildings:
- Damen: For 2637 N Damen, I’ve already labeled several samples, including the warehouse tile serial ASB-WHSE-2637NDAMEN-NONCOMPL-INSPECTOR-RICHARD-07-2022 from the first‑floor warehouse after my dad Richard’s July 2022 walkthrough, and FUNGUS-BSMT-2637NDAMEN-CMPLNT-UNIT1-TENANT-ER-09-18-2024 from the basement storage cage tied to the first‑floor tenant’s ER asthma visit at Rush.

Let’s use these as benchmarks when we look at the Lakeside boiler and roof assemblies.

– Michael

metadata.subject: Consolidated sampling notes before 3/5 Lakeside budget meeting

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Thompson <m.thompson@thompsonpmllc.com>
To: Mark Delaney <mark.delaney@cityofchicago.org>
Cc: Attorney Lisa Park <lpark@parkhousinglaw.com>
Date: March 2, 2026
Subject: Follow‑up documentation – prior violations, samples, and health complaints

Mark,

Per our July 2022 meeting in the office off the second‑floor corridor (south wall between Rooms 201 and 202 at the Damen Ave property) about the earlier asbestos violations and the $18,000 fine we negotiated down, I’m sending an updated snapshot of related sampling and complaints across my portfolio.

At 3119 W Armitage Ave, in Carlos Ramirez’s front office, the current lab sample is from the first‑floor south elevation, center bay steel window frame – the exact area cited in his May 2022 habitability complaint and the $3,800 in disputed rent now sitting in Cook County Housing Court with attorney Lisa Park.

At 1910 W Armitage Ave, we removed roughly 300 sq ft of gypsum board partition in the basement rec room in May 2021 after Luis in Unit 1F filed a leak/mold complaint. The City of Chicago housing inspector’s violation for suspected mold and water damage is in your system; our lab notes now explicitly tie that ~300 sq ft removal to the complaint.

For my mother, Margaret “Maggie” Thompson, I also documented approx. 280–300 sq ft of interior gypsum board partition we tore out of her bedroom at the Damen condo in October 2023 per her oncologist at Northwestern Memorial Hospital, to reduce potential mold/asbestos exposure during chemotherapy.

I’m trying to be proactive here so future inspections at 2637 N Damen, 2015 W North, and Lakeside aren’t happening in a vacuum.

Best,
Michael Robert Thompson
Owner / Managing Partner
Thompson Property Management, LLC

metadata.subject: Follow‑up documentation – prior violations, samples, and health complaints

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Thompson <m.thompson@thompsonpmllc.com>
To: Daniel Thompson <daniel@thompsonpmllc.com>
Cc: Erica Thompson <erica.thompson@example.com>
Date: March 2, 2026
Subject: Family health stuff + why I’m doubling down on sampling

Dan,

You’ve asked a couple of times why I’m “over‑doing it” on environmental testing, so here’s the short version tying our own family to the buildings.

Kids first: after Caleb’s elevated blood lead level in March 2022, DCFS sent an inspector to our old 3rd‑floor rental at 1842 W North Ave in Wicker Park. We ended up gutting approx. 300 sq ft of gypsum board partition in Sophie’s old bedroom there. Then in May 2022, at the first‑floor warehouse NW corner near the former office partition at 2637 N Damen, we pulled dust samples after Lurie Children’s flagged Caleb’s lead again – that’s the same general area we’d already been dealing with from the August 2021 flood and the Suite 1A tenant’s habitability complaint over incomplete mold remediation.

Fast‑forward: Sophie’s asthma has been flaring too. We took the BR-SOPHIE-2024-09-CHILD-EXPOSURE-CHECK boiler insulation sample from the boiler room at 2637 N Damen on 9/6/24 after an asthma episode, and Lurie just had us test peeling paint from the kids’ bedroom ceiling in Sophie’s room at 1842 W Cortland St after her elevated blood test on 2/6/25.

On the adult side, remember the basement mechanical room at 3510 W Armitage Ave where Kevin O’Donnell’s 9‑year‑old flooded the tub while Kevin was in rehab at Gateway Foundation in May 2023? That incident, plus the mold regrowth at 1842 W Evergreen after the March 2023 flood Allstate only partially covered (we fronted $18,000 from the Thompson Property Management, LLC LOC), convinced me we can’t keep reacting piecemeal.

So yeah, the sampling blitz is personal.

– Mike

metadata.subject: Family health stuff + why I’m doubling down on sampling

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Robert Thompson <m.thompson@thompsonprop.com>
To: Laura Chen <lchen@acm-engineering.com>
Date: 2025-03-10 8:42 AM
Subject: Consolidated sampling notes for ACM chain-of-custody

Hi Laura,

Per your request, I pulled together a single summary of the various residential and small-building samples we’ve taken so you can clean up the chain-of-custody forms.

At our home condo (2637 N Damen Ave, Unit 2F, Chicago), we’ve got multiple items:
- In May 2022, per Sophie’s pediatrician, Dr. Melissa Grant at the Lurie Children’s outpatient clinic, I opened the drop ceiling in Sophie’s former bedroom and removed roughly 4 linear feet of 2" hot water supply pipe insulation (Area/Volume/Material) for testing, since her asthma attacks were getting worse (MS7).
- In January 2024, after Mom (Margaret “Maggie” Thompson) slipped and hit her head in our master bedroom closet at 2637 N Damen, Unit 2F, we collected a mold sample from the closet ceiling above the north wall leak where the visible growth was (MS47).
- Also in January 2024, I opened about 300 sq ft of gypsum board partition between our living room and the small office we built out in Unit 2F. Emily’s allergist at Rush University Medical Center thought hidden mold might be triggering her asthma, so we had you test the wallboard and joint compound from that section (MS28).

Separately, at 2637 N Damen’s basement, we documented approx. 1,200 sq ft of 9" x 9" VAT in the laundry/utility area where Mom fractured her wrist on a loose tile in November 2023; you took a composite of 3 tiles plus mastic near the washer after we removed the broken piece (MS17).

Let me know if you need photos or floor plans tied to any of these.

– Michael

metadata.subject: Consolidated sampling notes for ACM chain-of-custody

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Robert Thompson <m.thompson@thompsonprop.com>
To: Daniel Thompson <dan.thompson@thompsonprop.com>
Cc: Erica Thompson <erica.thompson@example.com>
Date: 2025-03-11 2:07 PM
Subject: Family/portfolio risk cleanup – need your eyes

Dan,

I’m finally forcing myself to lay out some of the messier situations across the buildings so we can decide what to disclose, what to remediate, and what to just monitor.

Damen complex first: in the basement mechanical room under 2637 N Damen Ave, Unit 1R, we’ve got a couple of problem spots. One is the west wall return line near the floor drain where the small sewage backup hit on January 12, 2024 and Maria Alvarez threatened to call the City of Chicago Building Department on Thompson Property Management, LLC (MS3). Same general drain zone is where Mom’s November 2023 wrist fracture happened in the laundry/utility area with about 1,200 sq ft of 9" x 9" VAT; ACM took a composite of 3 tiles plus mastic there (MS17). In a different corner of that basement, we’ve used roughly 1,200 sq ft of 9" x 9" VAT as file-room flooring since January 2018 for tenant files and the 2019–2021 eviction/foreclosure folders; ACM sampled 3 tiles plus mastic under the cabinets (MS16).

We also need to remember the unpermitted basement studio at 2637 that you and Erica rented from March 2015 to August 2022 – about 1,200 sq ft of 9" x 9" VAT in that kitchenette where the City inspector wrote the violation. ACM took a composite of 3 tiles plus underlying mastic after we tore out the cabinets (MS15).

Let’s talk Friday about which of these need proactive disclosure before we list anything.

– M

metadata.subject: Family/portfolio risk cleanup – need your eyes

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Robert Thompson <m.thompson@thompsonprop.com>
To: Attorney Lisa Hernandez <lhernandez@hernandezlawchicago.com>
Date: 2025-03-12 9:18 AM
Subject: Consolidated incident & sampling background before Friday’s meeting

Lisa,

Ahead of Friday, here’s a consolidated snapshot of some of the higher‑risk incidents where we’ve already pulled environmental samples:

• 2637 N Damen Ave – first‑floor warehouse and loading dock: We sampled the first‑floor central warehouse bay at gridline C‑4 several times: tied to the Cook County health inspector’s March 2024 black‑mold citation and tenant eviction in Unit 1F that aggravated my daughter Sophie’s asthma during weekend visits (MS11); the same C‑4 zone is where my mom, Margaret “Maggie” Thompson, had a slip‑and‑fall on condensate in January 2024 and filed a premises liability claim (MS13); it’s also where we boxed up belongings from a former lead‑violation tenant who stopped paying rent in November 2023 and is now threatening to sue over Sophie’s alleged lead exposure (MS14). On the loading dock, we pulled a sample from the east wall near the overhead door where my dad, Richard Thompson, fell off a step stool and needed eight stitches at Northwestern Memorial on September 14, 2023 (MS44).

• 2637 N Damen – office corridor, south wall between Rooms 201 & 202: This corridor is central to multiple issues – your own October 17, 2023 mold‑injury consultation with me about a tenant claim (MS26); my mom’s June 2024 post‑hip‑replacement recovery setup after Northwestern Memorial (MS25); and the April 2024 workspace I improvised for my son Caleb’s remote tutoring during his three‑day suspension from Pulaski International School for a vaping incident (MS27).

I’ll bring the ACM Engineering lab packets and photos so we can map all of this to potential negligence exposure.

– Michael

metadata.subject: Consolidated incident & sampling background before Friday’s meeting
